Post_Text: The fire doesn't close your school. The eight months without a classroom does. Your property check rebuilds the wall. It does nothing about the eight months. Here's the coverage that does: [LINK]
The fire didn't close her school. The eight months without a classroom almost did. (Full newsletter, see linkedin-newsletter.md)

A pipe bursts over a holiday weekend. The rebuild takes eight months. Your families won't wait eight months. Where do the kids go on Monday, and who pays for it? This is the part nobody put in front of you: [LINK]

A coffee shop loses its building, customers come back when it reopens. A school loses its building, the funding walks out the door with the students. Same policy. Completely different stakes. Why the standard package quietly fails a charter school: [LINK]

Your property policy rebuilds the building. Two other lines decide whether the school survives the months it takes. Most school policies set them by guess. (Checklist + matrix, see company-page-checklist.md)
Notes: Post from company page; consider PDF lead-capture version

Post_Text: There's a coverage on your property policy built for the exact emergency a building loss creates. On most school policies it's the thinnest line on the page. It's called Extra Expense. Here's why yours is probably too small: [LINK]

Your Business Income limit is a number somebody picked years ago and never touched. It's renewed on autopilot ever since. Nobody ever tied it to your real payroll or how long you'd actually be down. Here's how to build that number instead of guess it: [LINK]

Your coverage assumed an eight-month rebuild. Permits, contractor backlog, and scarce materials made it fourteen. Guess who eats those last six months when the policy's clock runs out. The restoration period nobody showed you how to set: [LINK]

Picture your main building unusable tomorrow morning. Do you know how many months your current policy would keep this school operating, and how much it would pay to get students back in a room somewhere? Or has that limit just renewed itself for years? [LINK]
